journal_entry.description: Factory and office electricity charges paid via corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Factory electricity ($15,000) is a manufacturing overhead expense that flows to cost of goods sold, while office electricity ($5,500) is an administrative/operating expense. Both are paid via corporate credit card, increasing credit card payable liability. The debit tuple specifies 2 expense increases (c=2), matching the two distinct electricity expenses. The credit tuple specifies 1 liability increase (a=1), representing the credit card payable. Total debits ($20,500) equal total credits ($20,500). No tax applies as electricity utilities are generally billed with taxes already embedded or handled separately by the utility provider.

output.reason: Factory electricity charges ($15,000) = expense increase (manufacturing overhead/utility expense). Office electricity charges ($5,500) = expense increase (administrative/utility expense). Total: 2 debit lines, both are expense increases.

debit_classifier.reason: Factory electricity charges ($15,000) = expense increase (manufacturing overhead/utility expense). Office electricity charges ($5,500) = expense increase (administrative/utility expense). Total: 2 debit lines, both are expense increases.

output.reason: Payment made using corporate credit card creates a liability increase (credit card payable). Factory electricity ($15,000) and office electricity ($5,500) are both expenses being recorded, but on the credit side we only see the credit card liability increase - one line.

credit_classifier.reason: Payment made using corporate credit card creates a liability increase (credit card payable). Factory electricity ($15,000) and office electricity ($5,500) are both expenses being recorded, but on the credit side we only see the credit card liability increase - one line.
